spec_match.rationale: Implements the motivating chromestatus scenario verbatim — a sync loop that compares clipboardchange to focus-driven polling, calling readText() inside the handler.

interactive.rationale: Three strategy radios (event / 1s poll / focus poll), live metrics for reads / wasted reads / syncs, plus a per-event log.

blurb_alignment.rationale: Blurb promises a side-by-side cost comparison; the page exposes all three strategies and surfaces the wasted-read counter.

edge_cases.rationale: Covers happy + permission-denied + missing-event fallback, but never simulates a slow remote (back-pressure, retries) which is the real reason clipboard sync is hard in practice.

references.rationale: Links chromestatus and the spec PR in see-also, plus an inline link in the prose.

design_system.rationale: Variables only, no static card, button colours not overridden so global rule still applies.

openQuestions.title: Simulate remote latency / failure
openQuestions.detail: The remote VM is currently zero-latency and never errors. Add a slider (0-2000 ms) and a 'simulated network drop' toggle so visitors can see what happens when `sendToRemote` rejects — does the event-driven side miss state because there's no retry, while the poller eventually catches up? That trade-off is what determines whether to migrate.

openQuestions.title: Measure event coalescing for rapid copies
openQuestions.detail: If a user copies three things in 200 ms, the spec allows the UA to coalesce. The metrics row should expose 'events delivered vs system copies' so the visitor can see whether back-to-back copies all fire or whether only the last one does. Affects undo-stack design in editors.

openQuestions.title: Add a 'cost / minute' projection
openQuestions.detail: Wasted reads is a count but the headline insight is dollars / battery. A small derived metric — 'at 480 polls/hour over an 8-hour session: 3840 reads' — would make the case more concrete than a raw counter.

summary: The strongest concept under this feature — directly mirrors the chromestatus motivation and shows real metrics. To level up, model real-world failure modes (latency, drops, coalescing) since the page is otherwise arguing in a frictionless world.
